acts_1_2_levels_1_25.strategy: Chaos DoT leveler; same tree clusters as endgame; teaches curse tempo (Contagion primes, Drain detonates)
acts_1_2_levels_1_25.ascendancy_1_note: Start practicing life-cost management early; small costs teach the habit
acts_1_2_levels_1_25.why_this_phase: Zero respec cost at level 1-25; cluster on life and chaos damage; ascendancy is identity—take immediately

act_3_levels_26_40.strategy: Rehearse two-beat (Despair → Drain) before Hexblast arrives; introduce curse rhythm
act_3_levels_26_40.ascendancy_2_note: 15% base crit now; Drain doesn't crit much, but it's pre-investment for Hexblast
act_3_levels_26_40.curse_introduction: Despair reduces chaos res; queue it before Drain detonations; this is the future rhythm
act_3_levels_26_40.gear_note: Cap resistances; grab 60%+ life + resistances; spell damage is bonus

level_41_ignition.action: Engrave level 11 uncut gem (need 95 int by level ~38; check passives)
first_supports: Spell Critical Strike Chance (first slot—multiplies 15% base)
Increased Area of Effect (for chain overlap)
level_41_ignition.rotation_shift: Despair → Hexblast → move → repeat (not Despair → Drain anymore)
level_41_ignition.adjustment_period: 1-2 play sessions; rhythm is slower per cast but heavier per hit than DoT leveling
level_41_ignition.ascendancy_3_timing: Once Hexblast is online; this multiplies crit damage by life pool
level_41_ignition.gear_check: Weapon now matters; spell damage + crit chance + cast speed

late_maps: Flex slot: utility if survival needed

maps_levels_60_90.curse_setup: 1x Despair (pack AoE + duration); optionally 2x Despair variant (single-target, curse effect + duration for bosses)
maps_levels_60_90.ascendancy_4_note: Reserve enemy life + shut down regen; quietly a bossing multiplier
maps_levels_60_90.ascendancy_5_decision: Crimson: if gear budget growing (ES → life). Vitality: if squish (third recovery layer)
maps_levels_60_90.gem_level_track: Level 20 Hexblast by maps; level 1-5 supports scale gem level, not just support level

hexblast_links.reasoning: Multiplies 15% base; every crit node you take is now working on a respectable foundation
Stacks with Gore Spike; more-multiplier > increased; favor 'more' if choosing
Chain needs overlap; AoE widens both curse and explosion; scales pack-wide on 10-explosion cap
Accelerates two-beat rhythm; also speeds rotation between packs
Patch-dependent; check which offers most damage; or swap utility if survivability needed
Depends on gear/defense floor; if safe, damage; if risky, defense

despair_links.reasoning: Hit whole pack in one cast; chain needs multiple hexed targets to jump
Deepens Despair resistance reduction (hidden more-multiplier); deepest cut = most damage

gems: Curse Duration Support (if second Despair setup)
despair_links.reasoning: Bosses shrug curse duration; long-fight variant keeps Despair fresh

movement_skill.requirement: Non-negotiable; rhythm plants feet; time between casts = danger
movement_skill.note: 1-2 supports (usually speed or cooldown); exact gem is player preference

utility_buff.spirit_budget: Spend on flat damage to spells (scales Hexblast high added-damage effectiveness) or defense (life-adjacent)
utility_buff.note: Exact choice depends on gear/defense; prioritize flat chaos if budget allows

hexblast.level_20: Endgame standard; each level adds ~5% damage
hexblast.level_21_exceptional: If corrupted or quest reward; consider weapon upgrade before gem 21

supports.scaling: Support gem level scales support bonus + added damage; not just the gem's own bonus
supports.priority: Level supports to cap damage scaling; Hexblast benefit from level 20 supports is significant

skill_gem_notes.patch_sensitivity: Gem values shift; use in-game tooltip as source of truth, not guide numbers
skill_gem_notes.quality_scaling: Quality bonuses sometimes major (e.g. Hexblast quality rewards fresh curse detonation timing)
skill_gem_notes.corruption_path: Late-endgame: +1 gem level (gemstone) or +1 quality (divine vessel) are BiS upgrades
skill_gem_notes.defensive_swap: Despair → Enfeeble or Temporal Chains in hard content; same setup, different curse = different playstyle
